content.verdict: Capo d'Orso occupies one of the most dramatic locations on the Sardinian coast: its own granite peninsula above Palau, looking directly at the scattered islands of the La Maddalena archipelago. Eighty-five rooms step down the hillside through Mediterranean gardens to three small private coves, and the L'Incantu thalasso spa — built around marine water and Sardinian herbs — is consistently ranked among the best wellness experiences in Italy. For honeymooners who want quiet, scenery, and serious spa time without Costa Smeralda prices, this is a compelling alternative, with the ferry to La Maddalena island just 10 minutes down the hill.
content.best_room: A Prestige Sea View Room or the Junior Suite Sea View are the honeymoon picks — both have terraces framed by juniper and granite, with the archipelago laid out beyond. The Suite Le Ville with private garden is the upgrade choice; basic garden-view categories are worth skipping for a honeymoon given the setting.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival and archipelago sunset
itinerary_7_nights.description: Transfer from Olbia Costa Smeralda in about one hour. Check in to your sea-view room, aperitivo at the clifftop bar, and dinner at Gli Olivastri terrace as the sun drops behind La Maddalena.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Thalasso circuit and cove day
itinerary_7_nights.description: Start at L'Incantu spa — the thalasso marine pool, Sardinian herb hammam, and couple's treatment. Long lunch at the poolside restaurant, then down the path to Cala Selvaggia for a late-afternoon swim.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Private boat through La Maddalena
itinerary_7_nights.description: The concierge books a skippered gozzo from Palau harbor. Full day touring Spargi, Budelli's Pink Beach (viewed from offshore), and lunch anchored at Santa Maria. Back for a simple terrace dinner.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Palau village and Capo d'Orso rock
itinerary_7_nights.description: Morning walk to the famous bear-shaped Capo d'Orso granite formation above the hotel, lunch in Palau old town at Da Franco, afternoon at the hotel's second small cove, spa treatment before dinner.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Costa Smeralda day trip
itinerary_7_nights.description: Drive 40 minutes to Porto Cervo, browse the piazzetta, lunch at Il Pescatore, swim at Spiaggia del Principe. Return for sunset at the clifftop bar.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Maddalena town and Caprera
itinerary_7_nights.description: Ferry from Palau (10 min) to La Maddalena town, wander the pastel streets, continue by bridge to Caprera island for a walk through the pine forest to Cala Coticcio. Seafood lunch before returning.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Spa and farewell
itinerary_7_nights.description: Late breakfast, final thalasso circuit, last swim in the cove, and the car to Olbia. Many couples extend with two nights inland at a Gallura agriturismo or in Alghero.

caveats: The coves are small pebble-and-sand mixes accessed by stairs — stunning but not a long sandy beach. Day trips to Cala Brandinchi or Costa Smeralda beaches complement it well.
The hillside layout involves stairs and paths. Request a room close to the main building if mobility matters.
Open seasonally, generally May to October. Confirm dates before booking.

faqs.question: How good is the thalasso spa?
faqs.answer: L'Incantu is one of the top marine spas in Italy — large heated marine pool, several saunas and hammams, Sardinian herb and myrtle treatments. Allow at least two dedicated spa sessions in a week.

faqs.question: Is the beach directly at the hotel?
faqs.answer: There are three small private coves accessed by paths and stairs from the hotel, reserved for guests with sunbeds. For classic white-sand beaches, pair with shuttle or rental-car trips to Cala Brandinchi or Spiaggia del Principe.

faqs.question: How close is La Maddalena?
faqs.answer: The ferry port in Palau is a 5 to 10-minute drive downhill; ferries run every 30 minutes and take about 15 minutes to La Maddalena town.

faqs.question: Is it kid-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, it accepts families, but the atmosphere is generally calm and spa-forward. Peak August is busier; June and September are very honeymoon-friendly.

faqs.question: Do we need a car?
faqs.answer: Strongly recommended — the hillside location means a car greatly expands options to Costa Smeralda, Palau, and the northern beaches.

faqs.question: Which airport?
faqs.answer: Olbia Costa Smeralda (OLB), about one hour by road. The hotel arranges transfers on request.

content.email_template:

Buongiorno,

We are planning our honeymoon for [DATES] and would love to stay 6 to 7 nights at Capo d'Orso. Could you please send best-available rates for a Prestige Sea View Room and for a Junior Suite Sea View, so we can compare?

Also:
1. Is access to the L'Incantu thalasso circuit included in the rate, or priced separately?
2. Are any honeymoon packages available (champagne, couple's spa treatment, private cove aperitivo)?
3. Can you arrange a private skippered boat for a day through La Maddalena?
4. What are your current transfer rates from Olbia airport?

Thank you,
[Your names]
